title: Incorrectly Flipping Bits of a Number to Zero Count

statement.markdown: Given a non-negative integer `n`, return the minimum number of operations required to flip all the bits that are set to `1` in `n` to `0`. A bit is flipped by using bitwise XOR operation with `n`.
statement.inputFormat: A non-negative integer `n` as a string.
statement.outputFormat: The minimum number of operations required.
statement.notes: The input `n` will be in range `[0, 2^31 - 1]`.

examples.explanation: The binary representation of 5 is 101, so we need to flip the first and third bits.
The binary representation of 7 is 111, so we need to flip all the bits.

hints.text: Think about the properties of bitwise XOR operation.

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: The optimal solution uses a simple mathematical approach based on the properties of bitwise operations.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach could involve iterating over all the bits in the input number and keeping track of the number of bits that are set to 1.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use the property of bitwise XOR operation that `a ^ a = 0` and `a ^ 0 = a` to simplify the problem. We can also use the property that `a ^ b = b ^ a` to ensure that we are flipping the minimum number of bits.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the optimal solution works for all possible inputs.
